title: Test script only discovers two hard-coded globs, so new tests outside those folders are silently skipped
reasoning: The test command enumerates only top-level tests in src/lib and src/components/analysis. Any tests added under other common project areas, nested folders, app routes, hooks, utilities, or other components will not run when contributors or CI invoke npm run test. This creates a concrete regression risk because passing tests can falsely indicate full project test coverage while excluding newly added test files.
recommendation: Use a broader recursive test pattern that matches all project test files, such as src/**/*.test.ts and src/**/*.test.tsx, with any necessary exclusions for generated or fixture files.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The gap is in the test discovery command itself; tests outside the two hard-coded globs would not execute and therefore cannot reveal their own omission.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a trivial test file under another expected location, such as src/components/example.test.tsx or src/app/example.test.ts, and verify npm run test executes it before accepting the discovery pattern change.
minimumFixScope: Change package.json scripts.test to a recursive project-wide test discovery pattern.
